title: doronicum macrophyllum f. hyrcanum
intro: Doronicum macrophyllum f. hyrcanum, also known as Hyrcanian Leopard's Bane, is a subspecies of the plant Doronicum macrophyllum, which belongs to the dandelion-like flowering plant family Asteraceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating pain, and treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, the roots and leaves of Doronicum macrophyllum f. hyrcanum are typically used to make infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be consumed as teas or used topically to apply the antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties.
